title: Media Technical Directors/Managers
description: Coordinate activities of technical departments, such as taping, editing, engineering, and maintenance, to produce radio or television programs.

tasks.text: Switch between video sources in a studio or on multi-camera remotes, using equipment such as switchers, video slide projectors, and video effects generators.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe pictures through monitors and direct camera and video staff concerning shading and composition.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise and assign duties to workers engaged in technical control and production of radio and television programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor broadcasts to ensure that programs conform to station or network policies and regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Operate equipment to produce programs or broadcast live programs from remote locations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test equipment to ensure proper operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Train workers in use of equipment, such as switchers, cameras, monitors, microphones, and lights.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Act as liaisons between engineering and production departments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collaborate with promotions directors to produce on-air station promotions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Confer with operations directors to formulate and maintain fair and attainable technical policies for programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Schedule use of studio and editing facilities for producers and engineering and maintenance staff.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct technical aspects of newscasts and other productions, checking and switching between video sources and taking responsibility for the on-air product, including camera shots and graphics.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Follow instructions from production managers and directors during productions, such as commands for camera cuts, effects, graphics, and takes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Set up and execute video transitions and special effects, such as fades, dissolves, cuts, keys, and supers, using computers to manipulate pictures as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Discuss filter options, lens choices, and the visual effects of objects being filmed with photography directors and video operators.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
